plant_audit.P1: String of Nickels: Foliage remains consistent, showing signs of mild dehydration but stable turgor.
plant_audit.P2: Mexican Mint: Occupying the shared black pot; growth is slow but consistent with low-light adaptation.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos: Anchored by the white rabbit; one leaf shows significant chlorosis/necrosis at the margin, suggesting potential root stress or nutrient imbalance.
plant_audit.P4: Silver Guest: Shared with P2; shows a single, elongated, healthy-looking leaf emerging from the soil substrate.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistent moisture levels; no visible fungal blooms or surface crusting.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Presence of white perlite/debris on soil surface is consistent across all images.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-4: Minimal change; Pothos leaf necrosis remains static.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-2: Pothos leaf in P3 shows slight darkening of the necrotic margin.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_current: The P4 specimen shows a slight change in orientation, likely phototropic response to the fixed LED.

vision_report.anomalies: The Pothos (P3) leaf necrosis is the primary health concern; the rest of the biome appears to be in a state of stasis.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of low-metabolic activity. The Pothos (P3) is exhibiting signs of localized tissue death, likely due to environmental stress or moisture regulation issues. The other specimens (P1, P2, P4) are maintaining their baseline health. No new growth or systemic failures were observed during the five-day audit period.
